name: v1.2.0 — OpenSRS Registrar
body:
## v1.2.0 — OpenSRS (Tucows) Registrar Integration

Adds OpenSRS as a third supported domain registrar. OpenSRS is the original Tucows reseller platform, widely used by hosting providers and domain resellers worldwide.

### OpenSRS API
- Protocol: HTTPS POST, custom OPS XML envelope
- Auth: `X-Username` + `X-Signature` headers on every request
- Signature: `md5(md5(body + key) + key)` — double HMAC-MD5
- Sandbox: `horizon.opensrs.net:55443` (toggle per-module in Settings → Registrar)
- Production: `rr-n1-tor.opensrs.net:55443`
- Docs: https://domains.opensrs.guide/docs

### Commands Implemented
| Action | Object | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `LOOKUP` | `DOMAIN` | Single domain availability check (looped per TLD) |
| `SW_REGISTER` | `DOMAIN` | Register domain with contact_set + nameserver_list |
| `RENEW` | `DOMAIN` | Renew domain with currentexpirationyear |
| `TRANSFER` | `DOMAIN` | Initiate transfer with auth_info |
| `GET` (type=all_info) | `DOMAIN` | Sync status, expiry, nameservers |
| `ADVANCED_UPDATE_NAMESERVERS` | `DOMAIN` | Update nameservers (op_type=assign) |
| `GET` (type=auth_info) | `DOMAIN` | Retrieve EPP/auth code |
| `MODIFY_DOMAIN` (data=status) | `DOMAIN` | Lock/unlock transfer (lock_domain=0/1) |
| `MODIFY_DOMAIN` (data=whois_privacy_state) | `DOMAIN` | Enable/disable WHOIS privacy |

### Settings
- `opensrs_username` — OpenSRS reseller account username
- `opensrs_private_key` — OpenSRS private key (from Profile → API Access)
- `opensrs_sandbox` — Routes calls to `horizon.opensrs.net:55443`

All stored under the `registrar` settings group alongside ResellerClub and Enom keys.

### Settings UI
The Settings → Registrar tab now has a third panel for OpenSRS credentials, shown via Alpine.js `x-show` when OpenSRS is the selected module.

### Technical Notes
- XML envelope uses OpenSRS OPS protocol format with `<OPS_envelope>` root, `<header>` (version + XCP_version), and `<body>` containing `<data_block>` with `<dt_assoc>` attribute tree
- XML builder recursively encodes PHP arrays: indexed arrays → `<dt_array>`, associative arrays → `<dt_assoc>`, using `array_is_list()` (PHP 8.1+) to distinguish
- XML parser recursively decodes `dt_assoc`/`dt_array` nodes back to plain PHP arrays
- Signature calculated over the raw XML string body before sending
- Response success determined by `is_success === '1'` field in the parsed response
- Contact set: owner/admin/tech/billing all set to same registrant data
- Nameserver list built with sortorder starting at 1
